lines: * [FEATURE] Ingester: Add experimental `cortex_ingester_tsdb_head_chunks_max_mmapped` gauge reporting the maximum, across all per-tenant TSDBs, of the maximum number of head chunks memory-mapped for any individual series during the last memory-mapping pass. Temporary measurement metric; will be removed once we have collected enough data. #15616

* [FEATURE] kafkatool: Add `dump find-duplicates` command to scan an exported dump and report float samples re-sent with the same timestamp and value as the previous sample for a series. These exact duplicates are silently dropped by the ingester but still count toward received-samples metrics. An optional `--tenant` flag restricts the scan to a single tenant. #15506

* [FEATURE] Ingest storage: Add an experimental WarpStream-aware Kafka producer backend, enabled with `-ingest-storage.kafka.backend=warpstream`. It hedges and reroutes produce requests across WarpStream agents to reduce write tail latency, and is tuned via the experimental `-ingest-storage.kafka.warpstream-*` flags. #15236

-* [FEATURE] MQE: Add experimental support for running splitting and caching inside MQE, enabled with `-query-frontend.use-mimir-query-engine-for-splitting-and-caching-results=true`. #15348 #15393 #15397 #15650 #15720 #15750 #15769 #15783 #15787 #15795 #15804 #15822 #15823

+* [FEATURE] MQE: Add experimental support for running splitting, caching and spinning off subqueries from instant queries inside MQE. Enabled with `-query-frontend.use-mimir-query-engine-for-splitting-and-caching-results=true`. #15348 #15393 #15397 #15650 #15720 #15750 #15769 #15783 #15787 #15795 #15804 #15822 #15823 #15846

* [ENHANCEMENT] Store-gateway, Ingester: Add read support for XOR2 chunk encoding. XOR2 is a new Prometheus TSDB encoding that provides better compression than XOR, particularly for stale markers. #15371
* [ENHANCEMENT] MQE: Improve experimental support for reporting the number of samples read per query. #14838 #15179 #15191 #15220 #15223 #15232 #15237 #15255 #15276 #15282 #15285
* [ENHANCEMENT] Distributor: Relabel middleware returns early if neither label dropping nor relabeling is configured. #15246

Requires MQE, remote execution and sharding inside MQE to be enabled.",

[experimental] Set to true to enable performing query sharding inside the Mimir query engine (MQE). This setting has no effect if sharding is disabled. Requires remote execution and MQE to be enabled.

// WrapDownstreamQuery returns the expression to use in place of expr to execute it through the

+	// When the query has been rewritten to spin off subqueries, each __vector_evaluation_root__ or __scalar_evaluation_root__ marks a
+	// separate query that must be sharded independently. Some of these subtrees may be shardable and
+	// others not; that's fine, just like for top-level range queries today, where the shardable parts
+	// are sharded and the rest is left unchanged.
+			shardedChild, err := o.shard(ctx, tenantIDs, root.Args[0], options)
+// shard shards expr, returning the sharded expression, or expr unchanged if it cannot be sharded.
+func (o *OptimizationPass) shard(ctx context.Context, tenantIDs []string, expr parser.Expr, options requestoptions.Options) (parser.Expr, error) {
@@ -74,6 +97,30 @@ func (o *OptimizationPass) Apply(ctx context.Context, expr parser.Expr, _ types.
+// collectEvaluationRoots returns the __vector_evaluation_root__ and __scalar_evaluation_root__ marker function calls in expr.
+// Markers are never nested inside one another (the subquery spin-off mapper does not recurse into a
+// query once it has spun it off), so this does not descend into a marker once found.
+		if call, ok := node.(*parser.Call); ok && core.IsEvaluationRootFunctionCall(call) {

+		"multiple evaluation roots, some shardable and some not": {

+// mapTimeout bounds how long the subquery spin-off mapping is allowed to run, matching the timeout

+// Limits exposes the per-tenant configuration required by the subquery spin-off optimization pass.
+	// SubquerySpinOffEnabled returns whether spinning off subqueries from instant queries is enabled for
+// OptimizationPass spins off subqueries from instant queries as separate range queries, mirroring the
+// query-frontend subquery spin-off middleware but implemented as an AST optimization pass so that the
+// spun-off subqueries can be sharded, split, cached and executed remotely inside the Mimir query
+// It runs before the sharding optimization pass.
+func NewOptimizationPass(limits Limits, defaultStepFunc func(rangeMillis int64) int64, reg prometheus.Registerer, logger log.Logger) *OptimizationPass {
+func (o *OptimizationPass) Apply(ctx context.Context, expr parser.Expr, timeRange types.QueryTimeRange) (parser.Expr, error) {
+	// Subquery spin-off only applies to instant queries: range queries are left unchanged.
+	// Spinning off subqueries is opt-in, and is only applied when it is enabled for all tenants in the
+	spinOffQuery, ok := frontendspinoff.Map(ctx, expr, o.wrapper, o.metrics, o.defaultStepFunc, mapTimeout, spanLog)
+		// Spinning off subqueries was not possible or not worthwhile, so leave the query unchanged.

+		"spin off a subquery alongside a downstream query": {
+		"subquery range too short to spin off": {
